goals: Produce a valid, well-formed SVG file from any PNG input.
Emit a structured JSON object to stdout (mirroring `generate.py` conventions).
Keep the script self-contained under `scripts/trace/` with its own `package.json`.
Expose configurable tracing parameters via CLI flags.

userStories.title: Basic PNG → SVG conversion
userStories.description: As a developer or automated process, I want to run `node trace.js input.png` so that a valid SVG file is produced in the same directory as the input PNG.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `node trace.js input.png` exits with code `0` and writes a `.svg` file alongside the input PNG.
The output SVG is well-formed XML (parseable without errors).
If the input file does not exist, the script exits with code `2` and prints an error to stderr.
Nothing other than the JSON result is written to stdout.

userStories.title: Configurable tracing parameters
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to control tracing quality via CLI flags so that I can tune the output for different use cases.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `--colors <int>` sets the number of palette colors (default: `16`).
Invalid flag values (e.g. non-numeric) cause exit code `3` with an error on stderr.

userStories.title: Structured JSON output
userStories.description: As an automated process, I want the script to emit a JSON object to stdout so that the orchestrator can parse `svg_path` and `svg_inline` without reading files.

acceptanceCriteria.text: On success, stdout contains exactly one JSON object with at least: `png_path`, `svg_path`, `svg_inline`, `colors`, `tolerance`, `scale`.
`svg_inline` contains the full SVG markup as a string.
`svg_path` is the absolute path to the written SVG file.
On error, stdout is empty; the error message goes to stderr.

userStories.title: Documentation update
userStories.description: As a developer, I want project documentation to reflect the new tracing capability so that future contributors and agents have accurate context.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `README.md` is updated to mention `scripts/trace/` and its CLI usage.

`AGENTS.md` is updated: `trace.js` row in the script layout table filled in (no longer "planned"), and the unified CLI output JSON updated to note that `svg_path` and `svg_inline` are now produced by `trace.js`.
`.agents/PROJECT_CONTEXT.md` — `Modular Structure` and `Implemented Capabilities` sections updated to reflect iteration 000002 deliverables.
No other documentation files are modified beyond what is listed above.

functionalRequirements.description: ** The script is located at `scripts/trace/trace.js` and has its own `scripts/trace/package.json` declaring `imagetracerjs` as a dependency.
** The SVG output file is written to the same directory as the input PNG, with the same basename and `.svg` extension.
** stdout is reserved exclusively for the JSON result; all diagnostic messages go to stderr.
** Exit codes: `0` success · `1` tracing error · `2` input file not found · `3` invalid arguments.
** The script must work both when called directly (`node trace.js`) and when spawned as a subprocess.
** A `tests/` directory under `scripts/trace/` contains a test file (`test_trace.js` or similar) covering all acceptance criteria.
** On completion, `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, and `.agents/PROJECT_CONTEXT.md` are updated to reflect the new `scripts/trace/` module and its capabilities.
